title: Trochera colensoi
intro: Trochera colensoi, also known as Karamu or New Zealand holly, is a dioecious shrub native to New Zealand. The health benefits of Trochera colensoi include its potential use as an anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic agent, which may aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Trochera colensoi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Trochera colensoi include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Trochera colensoi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and is part of the tribe Podalyrieae. Within this tribe, Trochera colensoi is placed in the genus Trochera. This plant is native to New Zealand, and its classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. By studying its classification, botanists can gain insights into the evolutionary history and relationships of this species with other plants in the Fabaceae family.
